title: NIS2 Management Body Cybersecurity Training Evidence
exclusions: Does not store certificates or exam answers, does not certify legal applicability of NIS2 to your entity, and does not replace qualified legal review of board duties or national transposition measures.
evidence: Scheduled saved search `UC-22.2.41`, exported CSV of gap rows with `evidence_id`, dashboard panel screenshot, and optionally RFC 3161 timestamp token metadata archived alongside the weekly run.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.20 requires management body members to follow cybersecurity training. The UC tracks executive and board-member training completion dates against the governance roster. It proves training was attended and timestamped but cannot assess whether the training content satisfied the national transposition's knowledge requirements, hence partial.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.20 (Governance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.41: NIS2 Management Body Cybersecurity Training Evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.41' running on index=training (role=executive|board, course_code, completion_date), nis2_management_roster.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Fixture rows: `nis2_management_roster.csv` lists person_id exec-900 with board_member=Y and course alignment NIS2_EXEC; subsearch finds no lms:completion event — the alert returns gap_reason=missing_course and a non-null evidence_id.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Synthetic negative path: ingest two `lms:completion` events for person_id exec-900 with course_code=NIS2_EXEC, completion_date equal to the Friday twelve weeks before the test run, and score=94; keep appointment_date on the roster stable — the left-join brings last_completion forward, days_since stays at 84, gap_reason stays null, and the saved search result set is intentionally empty which is what we want for a clean annual attestation pack.

dataSources: `index=training` with `sourcetype=lms:completion` (LMS module completions keyed to `person_id`, `course_code`, `course_name`, `completion_date`, `score`, `role`, `department`). Authoritative membership and appointment metadata live in the CSV lookup `nis2_management_roster.csv` (`person_id`, `name`, `role`, `board_member`, `appointment_date`, `last_training_date`). Typical collectors: Splunk HTTP Event Collector (`inputs.conf` Global Settings) or the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 ([Splunkbase 4055](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055)) when the LMS is Entra ID–connected.
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 ([Splunkbase 4055](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055)) or native HTTP Event Collector (`http://`/`https://` token endpoint) for `lms:completion` events destined for `index=training`.
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_management_roster.csv
| eval roster_person=person_id
| join type=left roster_person [
    search index=training sourcetype=lms:completion (course_code="NIS2_EXEC" OR course_code="CYBER-BOARD-101") earliest=-730d
    | stats latest(completion_date) as last_completion latest(score) as last_score by person_id
    | rename person_id as roster_person
]
| eval last_completion_epoch=if(isnotnull(last_completion), strptime(last_completion,"%Y-%m-%d"), null())
| eval days_since=if(isnotnull(last_completion_epoch), round((now()-last_completion_epoch)/86400,0), null())
| eval gap_reason=case(isnull(last_completion),"missing_course",days_since>365,"stale_over_365d",true(),null())
| where isnotnull(gap_reason)
| eval evidence_id=md5(roster_person . "|NIS2_MGMT_TRAIN|" . if(isnotnull(last_completion), last_completion, "none"))
| table person_id, name, role, board_member, last_completion, last_score, days_since, gap_reason, evidence_id
description: Correlates the official management-body roster against `lms:completion` rows in `index=training` and surfaces people who never completed the mandated NIS2 governance curriculum or whose `completion_date` is more than 365 days old.
value: Produces repeatable training-gap evidence for board and supervisory conversations without manually reconciling spreadsheets each quarter, so legal and people teams can intervene before governance reviews or competent-authority questions materialise.
implementation: (1) Keep `nis2_management_roster.csv` aligned with corporate registry filings and `board_member` flags. (2) Publish stable `course_code` values from the LMS for NIS2 leadership modules. (3) Grant search-only RBAC (`srchIndexesAllowed` includes `training`) to HR compliance analysts; restrict roster lookup updates to `lookup_editor` service accounts. (4) Route alert output to the `audit_evidence` index or GRC tooling. (5) Pair with UC-22.2.20 for governance dashboards.
visualization: Table of roster rows with `gap_reason`, `days_since`, and `evidence_id`; single value for count of `stale_over_365d`; bar chart by `role`; timeline of `last_completion` per `person_id`.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Configure HTTP Event Collector

knownFalsePositives:
• **Azure AD provisioning lag after a board election** — Distinguish: Entra ID shows the new director but `person_id` in `nis2_management_roster.csv` still points to the predecessor. Suppress: time-bound exception row in `nis2_training_exceptions.csv` with `exception_until` covering the HR cut-over window.
• **Cornerstone OnDemand CSV replay duplicates completions** — Distinguish: identical `completion_date` with different `_cd` or source host indicates a collector resend. Suppress: filter the SPL subsearch with `| dedup person_id completion_date` before `stats latest()`.
• **Executive completes an alternate module with a different `course_code`** — Distinguish: HR confirms equivalent curriculum; events exist under `course_name` but not `NIS2_EXEC`. Suppress: extend the roster-driven `allowed_course_codes` column and `lookup` it to widen the subsearch `where` clause.
• **Sabbatical or medical leave documented only in Workday** — Distinguish: `department` still lists the person active but `board_member=yes` with leave paperwork. Suppress: exception register entry keyed on `person_id` referencing the leave ticket until return date.
• **Clock skew between LMS and Splunk makes `completion_date` appear future-dated** — Distinguish: `strptime` yields null or negative `days_since`. Suppress: add `where completion_date<=strftime(now(),"%Y-%m-%d")` after ingestion normalization in `props.conf`.
equipmentModels: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, task tracking for training follow-ups)
Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (lookups for board training register)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

• **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
• **Indexes**: `training` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
• **Lookups**: `nis2_management_roster.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('nis2_management_roster.csv')`.
• **Search-head capacity**: Subsearch-based detections need `subsearch_maxtime` ≥ 60s and `subsearch_maxout` ≥ 50000 in `limits.conf`. For high-volume environments, tune `[search] max_searches_per_cpu` up from default.
• **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.20`. The control objective is: "Evidence that NIS2 Art.20 (Governance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.41: NIS2 Management Body Cybersecurity Training Evidence." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is bespoke for this UC — no single canonical TA parses every source the detection relies on. Route data into `training` under `lms:completion`, and document the ingestion path in the UC runbook so future engineers know where each field originates.


5 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


• **Hint 1:** Keep `nis2_management_roster.csv` aligned with corporate registry filings and `board_member` flags. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 2:** Publish stable `course_code` values from the LMS for NIS2 leadership modules. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 3:** Grant search-only RBAC (`srchIndexesAllowed` includes `training`) to HR compliance analysts; restrict roster lookup updates to `lookup_editor` service accounts. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

• **Hint 4:** Route alert output to the `audit_evidence` index or GRC tooling. Routing targets (SOC, compliance team, specific queue) must be documented with named on-call owners; an 'alerts go to the team' runbook fails at 3am on Sunday.

• **Hint 5:** Pair with UC-22.2.20 for governance dashboards. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "NIS2 Management Body Cybersecurity Training Evidence," the aggregation is the evidence artifact itself — auditors will read these rows as the official record of NIS2 control activity.

This search correlates the outer dataset (index=`training`, sourcetype=`lms:completion`) with a subsearch on `roster_person` across the last 730 days. `type=left` preserves all rows from the outer search; nulls in the inner side indicate missing data that may itself constitute a finding.

Join caveat: Splunk's `| join` subsearch is capped at `max_searches_per_cpu * subsearch_maxout` results (default: 50000). If the inner subsearch exceeds this, rows are silently dropped and the resulting report will under-count. Check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd log_level=WARN subsearch` for truncation warnings. For inner searches expected to return >10000 rows, rewrite as `| stats` with a larger base search and post-filter, or use `| tstats` if a CIM datamodel covers the data.

Time-zone sensitivity: the `strptime(last_completion, "%Y-%m-%d")` conversion assumes the source timestamp is in the Splunk server's local time zone unless the format includes `%z`. If `last_completion` is emitted in UTC by the source system but Splunk's search head runs in a non-UTC zone, the resulting epoch will be off by the zone offset and the subsequent `where _time > last_completion_epoch` comparison will produce systematic false positives or negatives. Always validate by comparing `strftime(last_completion_epoch, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z")` against a known-good reference event before promoting to production.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.2.41: NIS2 Management Body Cybersecurity Training Evidence]
cron_schedule = 0 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -730d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.41,reg=NIS2
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=training sourcetype=lms:completion earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `person_id`, `name`, `role`, `board_member`, `last_completion`, `last_score`, `days_since`, `gap_reason`, `evidence_id`. Run:

```spl
index=training sourcetype=lms:completion earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table person_id, name, role, board_member, last_completion, last_score, days_since, gap_reason
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(d) Threshold calibration:** Before enabling as a production alert, run the base search with the threshold removed and profile the distribution of `days_since`:

```spl
<base search> earliest=-30d
| stats perc50(days_since) as p50 perc90(days_since) as p90 perc99(days_since) as p99 max(days_since) as hi
```

The current threshold `365` should sit between p90 and p99 for a balanced signal-to-noise ratio. If it's below p50, expect heavy alert volume; if above p99, expect under-detection.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.2.41: NIS2 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Table Of Roster Rows With `Gap Reason` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — `Days Since` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — And `Evidence Id`; Single Value For Count Of `Stale Over 365D`; Bar Chart By `Role`; Timeline Of `Last Completion` Per `Person Id` (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**No results when violations exist:** The most likely cause is subsearch truncation (see Step 2). Inspect `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=searchprocessor log_level=WARN subsearch` for messages like 'Output result count exceeded 50000.'

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIS2-specific failure modes:**

• 24/7 reporting obligation (Art. 23): NIS2 requires early warning within 24 hours of awareness of a significant incident. Detections that back this SLA must compute elapsed time from the SOC's first-acknowledged timestamp (not the resolve-time) and must trigger independently of business hours — the regulatory clock does not pause overnight.

• Member-state variation: NIS2 is transposed individually by each EU Member State with some latitude on thresholds and sector scoping. A detection correct for German IT-SiG 3 (BSI) may be misaligned with Dutch CWV or French LPM. Use `nis2_member_state_config.csv` to parametrize thresholds per jurisdiction; never hardcode.

• Essential vs Important entity criteria: the applicable obligations depend on sector + size. Before alerting, verify the monitored entity's designation; a detection firing on an out-of-scope subsidiary causes unnecessary regulatory notifications that erode trust with the national CSIRT.

• Supply-chain (Art. 21(2)(d)) extent: NIS2 explicitly includes direct suppliers' cybersecurity posture. Detections must cover monitored-by-you-not-owned-by-you infrastructure (outsourced SaaS, managed SIEM tenants) — these are in scope even if operationally run by a third party. BA/BAA-style governance applies.
grandmaExplanation: We check an official list of leaders against training records so we can see who still needs the yearly cyber lesson the law expects directors to take, before anyone important is left uncovered.
